mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0 percent, weak labor market conditions suppress consumer spending and corporate profit growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >65% of months where UNRATE > 9.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) after 2011.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or forward returns are negative in >55% of quarters when both series show positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production above 95.0 signals capacity utilization approaching historical peaks, triggering short-term positive market momentum as investors price in near-term earnings beats over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during supply-constrained periods, or 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A falling unemployment rate combined with near-zero policy rates confirms a soft landing trajectory, encouraging equity risk premium compression and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models, or forward returns are negative when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth directly maps to corporate earnings revisions, creating a persistent positive correlation between macroeconomic output expansion and S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 5-year rolling window, or GDP growth fails to predict return direction in >50% of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 220.0, rising inflation expectations force markets to price in tighter monetary policy, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of months when CPIAUCSL > 220.0, or the signal loses significance after 2012.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls exceeding 131000 confirms labor market tightening, which supports consumer spending and corporate profit margins, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal flips to negative during stagflationary periods, or forward returns are negative in >55% of triggered months.
